followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: No material change to overall assessment or verdict. My prior-cycle objections hold up on re-examination. Slight downward adjustment to overall score (7.8 → 7.5) to reflect Codex's valid point that the approximate factorizations (1D transfer function, diagonal covariance rescaling) are validated only via simulations whose full output cannot be audited from the truncated bundle. No upward adjustment toward Gemini's more generous position is warranted.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Gemini's reviewer_confidence of 9/10 is unreasonable for a paper where ~62% of the text (including all of Section VII and most appendices) is truncated. High confidence requires auditing the full cosmological analysis.
Gemini listing zero missing_assumptions and zero needs_human_spotcheck for a paper this complex is implausible and reflects under-scrutiny rather than genuine absence of concerns.

Gemini's technical_soundness of 9 does not adequately account for the disclosed issues: post-unblinding corrections detected at 3.5–6σ, TT null-test failures requiring post-hoc re-thresholding, covariance regularization needed to pass conditional tests, and a retained Polspice configuration error. These are disclosed limitations, not disqualifying, but they lower soundness below 9.

Codex's classification of the factorization approximations and covariance regularization as 'would_change_verdict_if_true' is overstated: these techniques are industry-standard, validated via large simulation suites, and consistent with published ACT/Planck methodology. Failure of these approximations would be surprising at the level needed to flip a verdict.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: Codex correctly flags that the approximate decoupling of filtering/mask/covariance effects is validated by simulation, but those simulations are not fully auditable from the truncated bundle — a fair methodological caveat.
Codex correctly notes that missing numeric values in the bundle are a rendering artifact, not a paper flaw, but they do constrain what can be independently verified.
Codex's observation that the covariance regularization needs to be validated for coverage (not just chi-square behavior) is a legitimate additional concern beyond what I raised in cycle 1.

main_claims.claim: SPT-3G D1 maps are the deepest used in a CMB TT/TE/EE analysis to date, with coadded white noise ~2.4 μK-arcmin (T) and ~3.4 μK-arcmin (P).
main_claims.notes: Noise levels at individual frequencies and coadded levels are quoted explicitly and cross-checked via noise power spectra and SNR comparison (Fig. 11).
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract; Section II; Section IV.5; Fig. 10

main_claims.claim: SPT-3G D1 provides the most precise lensed EE measurements at ℓ=1000–4000 and lensed TE measurements at ℓ=1000–3000.
main_claims.notes: SNR comparison with Planck and ACT DR6 in Fig. 11 supports both claims. TE at ℓ=1000–2000 is acknowledged as roughly equivalent to ACT DR6.

main_claims.claim: CDM parameters from SPT-3G alone are consistent with Planck and ACT DR6.
main_claims.notes: Consistency asserted and shown in Table 1/Fig. 2, but numerical values are not rendered in the truncated bundle; independent verification from supplied text alone is not possible.

main_claims.claim: For the first time, combined ground-based CMB experiments (SPT+ACT) reach Planck constraining power on some parameters including H0.
main_claims.notes: Plausible given demonstrated sensitivity levels, but the detailed parameter comparison is in truncated sections. The claim depends on how 'Planck constraining power' is defined and which lensing datasets are included.

main_claims.claim: A ~2.8σ tension between CMB (SPT+ACT+Planck) and DESI DR2 BAO exists in ΛCDM in the rd·h–Ωm h² plane.
main_claims.notes: The tension level is stated but the methodology (Section VII.3) is truncated. Consistency with other published CMB vs DESI-DR2 comparisons increases plausibility.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract; Section II, point 6; Section VII.3 (truncated)

main_claims.claim: Gaussian-constrained inpainting of point sources reduces estimator variance by ~33% relative to masking.
main_claims.notes: Covariance ratio computed from 500 Fullmock simulations. Fig. 8 clearly shows ~50% increase from masking vs. ~10% from inpainting. Methodology follows established CMB lensing practice.

main_claims.claim: Post-unblinding changes (quadrupolar beam leakage, polarized beam model) do not materially affect cosmological results.
main_claims.notes: The corrections are non-trivial (leakage at 3.5–6σ; beam model with free nuisance parameters clearly preferred by data). Impact on cosmological results is asserted small but Appendix B is in the truncated portion.
main_claims.paper_location: Sections IV.2.2, IV.2.3; Appendix B (truncated); Section V note

missing_assumptions.assumption: The one-dimensional transfer-function approximation is sufficient to capture all anisotropic filtering effects, with only diagonal covariance rescaling needed (Eq. 13: T²_F ≈ T_F²).
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: This binary-filter argument (Eq. 13) is plausible but applied to an inhomogeneous scan strategy; any residual off-diagonal mode coupling propagated into the covariance could bias parameter constraints and goodness-of-fit, especially for TE at high ℓ.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The foreground phenomenological model is adequate within the baseline multipole range, with robustness tested in Appendix E.2 (truncated).
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: At this sensitivity level, residual foreground mismodeling could shift parameters sensitive to high-ℓ TT (e.g., ns, Neff). The robustness appendix cannot be reviewed.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The Gaussian band-power likelihood approximation (Eq. 39) is valid across the full multipole range ℓ≥300 for a 4% sky fraction.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: For small sky fractions the number of modes per bin at lower ℓ may be insufficient for Gaussianity to hold, potentially biasing credible intervals near ℓ~300.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Super-sample lensing is adequately treated with a Gaussian prior σ(κ̄)~0.003 derived from prior SPT analyses assuming Planck ΛCDM cosmology.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If the true cosmology differs from Planck ΛCDM (as suggested by the DESI tension), this prior may be biased, potentially shifting σ8 or AL at the ~0.1σ level.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do the post-unblinding changes (quadrupolar beam leakage correction, polarized beam model) constitute a protocol violation, or were they anticipated in the pre-registered blinding plan? Appendix B is truncated.
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Appendix B documenting the post-unblinding decision record is beyond the 140,000-character truncation point. Whether these corrections were pre-listed as allowed post-unblinding additions cannot be assessed from the supplied text.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the reported cosmological parameter constraints (Table 1, Section VII) and the 2.8σ CMB–DESI tension (Section VII.3) robust to the post-unblinding corrections and correctly computed using an appropriate multivariate tension metric?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Section VII is largely truncated. The tension calculation methodology and extended model results (Section VII.4) cannot be reviewed.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the covariance matrix regularization (α=0.01, Eq. 38) preserve correct frequentist coverage in end-to-end simulations for all extended cosmological models in Section VII, not just ΛCDM?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The validation of the regularization across extended models would appear in Section VII and related appendices, which are truncated. Coverage diagnostics are not accessible.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does SPT-lite faithfully preserve constraining power and unbiased posteriors for extended models (Neff, Ωk, w0/wa), or are there compression losses that affect the tension characterizations?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Appendix F on SPT-lite performance and Section VII.4 extended model results are in the truncated portion.

novelty_assessment: Novelty is high by observational CMB standards. The primary contribution is the deepest CMB dataset yet used in a T&E analysis, enabling the most precise EE/TE band powers at high multipoles and the milestone of ground-based CMB (SPT+ACT) matching Planck precision. Methodological novelties include: (1) first application of Gaussian-constrained inpainting to primary CMB T&E analysis; (2) curved-sky HEALPix pipeline replacing flat-sky approximation for SPT; (3) Quickmock fast simulation framework; (4) differentiable JAX-based candl likelihood enabling gradient-based robustness testing; (5) an updated Camphuis et al. semi-analytic covariance. The characterization of the CMB–DESI DR2 tension adds cosmological significance. This is field-advancing work, not incremental.
score_rationale: Technical soundness (7): Pipeline is sophisticated and generally well-justified with large simulation suites. Deductions for: non-trivial post-unblinding corrections to beam leakage and polarized beams; covariance matrix requiring regularization to pass conditional tests; formal TT null-test failures with post-hoc re-thresholding; retained Polspice configuration error. None individually fatal, but together they reflect real precision-level systematics that the initial pipeline missed. Novelty (7): Primarily observational, but with genuine methodological contributions (inpainting, curved-sky pipeline, differentiable likelihood, Quickmock). The SPT+ACT milestone and CMB–DESI tension characterization add scientific novelty. Significance (9): Landmark result — deepest ground-based CMB dataset, first SPT+ACT matching Planck precision, new quantitative evidence on the Hubble and DESI tensions, publicly released data and likelihood. Widely cited and used. Clarity (8): Well-organized, thorough in disclosing assumptions and limitations; dense but navigable. Reviewer confidence (6): Source text truncated at ~38% of total characters, preventing review of the full cosmological analysis, extended model results, and most appendices. Overall 7.5 reflects a strong, well-executed major observational paper with manageable but real technical caveats and limited review scope.
summary: SPT-3G D1 is a landmark observational paper presenting CMB TT/TE/EE power spectra from 2019–2020 SPT-3G Main-field observations (~4% sky). Maps are the deepest used in a CMB T&E analysis to date. Key methodological advances include: curved-sky HEALPix pipeline; Gaussian-constrained inpainting (first application to primary CMB T&E); Quickmock/Fullmock simulation chain; JAX-based differentiable candl likelihood; and a semi-analytic covariance framework. Results are consistent with Planck and ACT DR6 in ΛCDM; SPT+ACT reaches Planck-level precision on some parameters for the first time; a ~2.8σ CMB–DESI DR2 BAO tension is reported. The paper discloses non-trivial post-unblinding corrections (quadrupolar beam leakage detected at 3.5–6σ; polarized beam model with free sidelobe fraction) and several TT null-test failures that were judged acceptable. The source bundle is truncated at ~38% of total characters, preventing review of the full cosmological analysis, extended models, and most appendices. My prior-cycle assessment is largely unchanged; the other reviewers contribute overlapping but not materially new objections, and one reviewer is unreasonably generous given the truncation.

technical_objections.evidence: Section IV.2.2: 'the quadrupolar leakage correction was implemented only after unblinding.' Section IV.2.3: 'the baseline polarized beam model was implemented only after unblinding.' Leakage amplitudes are 3.5–6σ detections (Eqs. 15–17).
technical_objections.objection: Non-trivial post-unblinding changes undermine the blinding protocol. Quadrupolar beam leakage (detected at 3.5–6σ) and a polarized beam sidelobe model (with free parameters, 'clearly preferred by the data') were both identified and implemented after unblinding. Both affect the band powers used for cosmological inference.

technical_objections.evidence: Section IV.6: 'we fail our more stringent conditional tests because the associated error bars are much smaller than the sample variance error bars. We acknowledge this limitation of our covariance approximation and regularize the matrix.' α=0.01 reduces average χ² by 60 points.
technical_objections.objection: Covariance matrix regularization is required to pass conditional consistency tests, indicating unmodeled inter-frequency systematic effects at the ~1% sample-variance level.

technical_objections.evidence: Azimuth null test at 95 and 150 GHz, and year null test at 95 GHz exceed the pre-determined PTE threshold. Justification invokes amplitudes '<5% of sample variance' as an acceptable threshold, but this criterion is introduced after the fact.
technical_objections.objection: Several TT null tests formally fail the pre-defined threshold. The post-hoc justification that contamination is <5% of sample variance was not pre-registered as a passing criterion.

technical_objections.evidence: Section IV.1.2: 'The TE and EE transfer functions differ very slightly from the TT transfer function at high multipoles due to a Polspice configuration error discovered after initial paper submission. We continue to use the originally calculated versions for consistency.'
technical_objections.objection: A Polspice configuration error produced slightly incorrect TE and EE transfer functions that were retained in the final analysis for consistency rather than corrected.

technical_objections.evidence: Section IV.3: 'The assumption of uncorrelated frequencies induces biases in the cross-frequency spectra that we estimated.' Residual bias is subtracted via simulation templates (Fig. 4), but the calibration uncertainty is not separately quantified.
technical_objections.objection: Inpainting makes three compounding approximations (small constraining region, frequency-independent inpainting, bundle-shared coadd inpainting), and the second explicitly introduces cross-frequency spectrum biases calibrated via simulations whose accuracy at this precision level cannot be verified from the supplied text.
